noted_behavior: Baseline configuration (SB3 default-like values)
Higher lr may speed up early learning but risks instability
Lower lr should be more stable but slower to converge
Smaller batch -> noisier gradient estimates
Larger batch -> smoother gradients and more compute per step
Even larger batch: expect diminishing returns vs compute cost
Combo: high lr + large batch to offset gradient noise
Combo: low lr + small batch - expect slow/underfit learning
SB3-Zoo-style lr for comparison against our baseline

Baseline configuration (same as Chol exp1 for a shared reference point)
Lower gamma -> more short-sighted agent and may miss long rallies
Moderate gamma reduction
Very high gamma -> values distant future reward almost equally
Slower epsilon decay -> prolonged exploration
Faster epsilon decay -> agent exploits sooner and risks a local optimum
Higher epsilon floor -> never fully stops exploring
Fully greedy at end of decay (epsilon_end=0)
Combo: reduced gamma + slower epsilon decay
Combo: high gamma + higher exploration floor
